key_models.ir.module.module: Module state and version tracking. Fields: name, state, latest_version, installed_version.
key_models.ir.model.data: XML-ID mapping (module, name, model, res_id, noupdate). Critical for external_id preservation during migration.
key_models.ir.config_parameter: System parameters including version info, migration flags, and custom settings.
key_models.ir.cron: Scheduled actions to run post-migration or validation jobs.
key_models.res.users: User accounts and group assignments requiring validation after migration.
key_models.res.company: Company records for multi-company isolation validation.
key_models.upgrade.analysis: OpenUpgrade analysis model storing module coverage and change analysis.

key_files.base/models/ir_model.py: Registry and model metadata management during migration
key_files.openupgradelib/openupgrade.py: Library helpers: migrate_vals, rename_columns, add_fields, map_values

crons.custom_post_migration_cron: User-defined cron for post-migration validation or cleanup

security_groups.base.group_no_one: Technical Features — access to technical fields and menus
security_groups.base.group_user: Internal User — standard access after migration

record_rules.multi_company_rule: [('company_id','in',company_ids+[False])] — applies to many models after migration

best_practices: Always backup database before any migration attempt
Use pre-migration scripts for schema changes and column renames
Use post-migration scripts for data transformation and computed field recomputation
Use end-migration scripts for finalization tasks after all modules loaded
Preserve XML-IDs to maintain data references across versions
Test migration in a staging copy of production before cutover
Run OpenUpgrade analysis to identify covered modules and required custom scripts
Validate financial balances and workflow states after migration
Document rollback steps and maintain snapshot restore capability
Keep migration scripts idempotent where possible
